cards.back: A linear data structure that follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle, where elements are added from one end and removed from the other end.
Queue operations include enqueue (add element), dequeue (remove element), peek (view front element), and is empty (check if queue is empty)?

cards.front: How to implement Queue using Arrays in Java?
cards.back: Using an array of size n, we can implement a queue by maintaining two pointers, front and rear, to keep track of the front and rear elements of the queue.
A queue where the last element is connected to the first element, forming a circular structure, allowing us to overwrite the front element when the rear element is full.
A queue that allows elements to be added and removed from both the front and rear ends, also known as a double-ended queue ordeque.
